title: Use specific Chinese language metadata when script or region matters

detect.signal: Web-facing Chinese Text is scoped by bare `zh` language metadata, such as `<html lang="zh">`, without a project override or nearby metadata that identifies script or region.
ignore_when: The text is not rendered to end users, such as source comments, internal documentation, debug logs, or synthetic fixtures.
A Project Override documents that bare `zh` maps to a project default script or region for this surface.
A closer element, route, localized resource, or content record already provides a more specific language tag for the Web-facing Chinese Text.
The content is intentionally script-neutral and no active rule depends on script or region.
The review task is only about generic markup validity and not about Chinese text handling.

rationale: Bare `zh` identifies Chinese but does not tell an agent whether script or regional conventions matter. When Chinese text guidance depends on `Hans`, `Hant`, or a region, the agent should ask for or suggest more specific language metadata instead of assuming it.

web_platform.title: BCP 47: Tags for Identifying Languages
web_platform.section: Language tag syntax, subtags, and matching

web_platform.title: W3C Internationalization: Language tags in HTML and XML

examples.context: Simplified Chinese UI copy with no project default locale.
Traditional Chinese UI copy scoped on a component.

agent_guidance.comment_template:
[advisory] clreq.metadata.lang-specificity: The page uses a bare `zh` language tag for Web-facing Chinese Text.

影响： Some Chinese text recommendations depend on script or region, and the agent should not assume Simplified or Traditional Chinese from `zh` alone.
Suggested change: Confirm the intended locale and use a more specific BCP 47 language tag such as `zh-Hans` or `zh-Hant` when appropriate.
Source: HTML Standard; BCP 47; W3C Internationalization language tag guidance.
Confidence: medium
agent_guidance.autofix_notes: Do not replace bare `zh` automatically. If the user or Project Override identifies the intended script or region, an agent may update language metadata when explicitly asked to make changes.
agent_guidance.decision_notes: If locale intent is unknown, keep the finding advisory and ask for a human decision. If a project-wide default locale already resolves `zh`, mention the override or suppress the suggestion according to project policy.
